milestones.title: Further reduce Haiku 4.5 synthesis time in async Lambda (currently 23.4s avg)
milestones.description: Two-phase synthesis shipped (PR #156, Mar 31). Phase 1 streams Key Points to DynamoDB as partial result (~8s). Client polls for Key Points while Phase 2 generates full answer (~18s). Down from 23.4s full wait. Further optimization: compact prompt active (SYNTHESIS_PROMPT_COMPACT=1). Lambda handler + polling API updated.
milestones.quickPrompt: Two-phase synthesis (PR #156) ships Key Points in ~8s, full answer ~18s. Compact prompt active. Lambda + polling API updated.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Further Optimize Evidence Engine Haiku Synthesis Speed

**Repo:** sevaro-evidence-engine
**Key files:** src/lib/bedrock-converse.ts, src/lib/evidence-tools.ts

## Current State (Mar 30, 2026)
- PR #140: Compact synthesis prompt reduced avg Lambda from 27.4s to 23.4s (-14.6%). SYNTHESIS_PROMPT_COMPACT=1 set permanently on Lambda.
- PR #136: Parallel validation ships synthesis to user before validation completes — improves perceived latency.
- Benchmark: KB=5 result count had no effect on complex queries (28.7s avg, +1.3s vs baseline).
- Confirmed bottleneck: Sonnet tool-calling loop, not Haiku synthesis or KB retrieval.
- performanceConfig NOT supported for Haiku 4.5 in us-east-2.

## Investigation areas
1. Parallelize Sonnet reasoning steps (currently sequential tool-call rounds)
2. Reduce number of tool-calling round-trips for common queries
3. Investigate streaming synthesis tokens to client during Lambda execution
4. Profile CloudWatch logs for largest context sections in tool-calling phase

## Acceptance criteria
- Complex queries synthesize in under 15s total Lambda time
- No regression on simple queries (currently ~8s)
- All existing tests pass
